operationName: Operation Swords of Iron (Israel) / October 7 War
heroHeadline: UN: Gaza Ceasefire 'Increasingly Fragile'; 824 Killed Since October 2025 Truce
heroSubtitle: UN Assistant Secretary-General Khiari briefed the Security Council on April 28 that the Gaza ceasefire is 'increasingly fragile' — 824 Palestinians (including 200+ children) killed since October 2025 truce began, 1.8M remain displaced. April 28 strikes killed at least 5 Palestinians including 9-year-old Adel al-Najjar in Khan Younis. Al Jazeera documents: under cover of ceasefire, Israel now controls ~60% of Gaza Strip, has expanded military perimeter 37 km east, and blocked the NCAG governance body. Phase 2 disarmament talks remain deadlocked; total death toll at 72,587+ since Oct 7, 2023.
footerNote: Data sourced from IDF, Hamas, UN OCHA, UNRWA, WHO, ICJ, ICC, Times of Israel, and Haaretz. Gaza death toll: 72,587+ confirmed killed since Oct 7, 2023 (Gaza Ministry of Health / WAFA). 824 killed since Oct 10, 2025 ceasefire framework per OIC Palestine Observatory (Apr 28, 2026). ICC arrest warrants issued for Netanyahu and Gallant (Nov 2024). Board of Peace established Jan 2026 under UN Resolution 2803. UN ASG Khiari (Apr 28, 2026): ceasefire 'increasingly fragile'; 1.8M displaced. UN Women (Apr 25, 2026): 38,000+ women and girls killed Oct 2023–Dec 2025 (avg. 47/day). EU-UN Joint Recovery and Reconstruction Assessment (Apr 21, 2026): $71.4B needed over 10 years; $35.2B infrastructure damage; 371,888 housing units damaged/destroyed; human development set back 77 years. OCHA Apr 23 report: aid pallets improving (17,400/week Apr 14–20) but still 37% of required levels. 2026 Flash Appeal $4.1B req, only 9.7% funded. Palestinian municipal elections in Deir al-Balah and West Bank (Apr 25–27, 2026): Fatah sweeps; Hamas excluded; 23% turnout. Hamas formally rejected Board of Peace disarmament plan (Apr 14, 2026); Phase 2 ceasefire deadlocked. Al Jazeera weekly wrap (Apr 28–29): Israel controls ~60% of Gaza, expanded control zone 37 km east since ceasefire; NCAG governance body 'emptied of its role.'
